signals.title: USD/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Stress
signals.text: USD/AUD at 1.4021 (inverse 0.7132) — G8 scorecard confirms a dominant USD lead (+0.579%) against AUD laggard (-0.904%) as global bond yields explode following the underwhelming Trump-Xi summit. Geopolitical escalation via a drone strike on a UAE nuclear plant drives safe-haven demand for USD while weighing on risk-sensitive AUD. Despite a -60bp carry deficit (Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%), the 1.484% G8 spread indicates macro-driven USD outperformance. Implication: Risk-off flows and yield volatility override carry advantages; AUD/USD downside pressure persists toward 0.7100.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Options + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.92 — 283bp OIS carry (SOFR 3.56% vs TONA 0.73%) keeps the pair bid despite extreme speculative positioning. JPY COT LF net short -70,605 (33.1% long) indicates a crowded carry trade, while the options market signals acute caution with a strong 25d RR put skew of -1.73. This divergence between spot bid and downside hedging reflects fear of BoJ intervention as price holds near the 160.00 threshold. Implication: Institutional hedging for a sharp reversal despite the carry-driven uptrend. Watch for verbal intervention or a break of 160.00.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5838 — COT LF net short -19,437 (22.3% long) shows specs fading the RBNZ hike bias ahead of the 27 May meeting. This institutional short is met by an extreme retail long of 87%, creating a dual-layered contrarian risk. While the services sector contraction (PSI) weighs on the bird, the OIS market prices a hike bias that is likely to trigger a massive short-squeeze if delivered. Implication: Retail capitulation risk if 0.5800 breaks, but high squeeze potential on hawkish RBNZ delivery. Watch RBNZ meeting 27 May.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + Positioning
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3314 — IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 7.4% generates an elevated +3.6% Vol Risk Premium (VRP), signaling the options market is paying a significant premium for protection relative to realized movement. GBP COT LF net long remains high at +40,980 (70% long), suggesting a crowded institutional long. With UK employer confidence at record lows and a -17bp OIS carry deficit vs USD, the high VRP suggests the market is bracing for a volatility spike or trend reversal. Implication: Expensive protection and crowded longs signal late-cycle exhaustion. Watch for 1.3300 support break.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Complacency + Carry
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7869 — Pair supported by a massive +375bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%). IV at 10.6% vs HV30 at 7.2% results in a +3.4% VRP, indicating that despite the steady carry-driven climb, the options market is pricing in tail-risk protection for a CHF safe-haven bid. Implication: Carry dominates the spot trend, but the elevated VRP suggests institutional fear of a rapid risk-off deleveraging event. Watch VIX; if VIX > 25, carry support will likely fail.
